selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":{"age":65,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"electrician","city_of_living":"Bari","employment_status":"employed part-time","education_level":"lower secondary","parents_education":{"parent_1":"primary school","parent_2":"no formal education"},"current_marital_status":"married","children":1,"migration_status":"native-born Italian","psychological_feature":"curious","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media","hobbies":["painting","reading novels","classical music","photography"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":77,"level":"high"},"conscientiousness":{"score":76,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":94,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":12,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":99,"level":"high"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = human
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = {"age":65,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"electrician","city_of_living":"Bari","employment_status":"employed part-time","education_level":"lower secondary","parents_education":{"parent_1":"primary school","parent_2":"no formal education"},"current_marital_status":"married","children":1,"migration_status":"native-born Italian","psychological_feature":"curious","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media","hobbies":["painting","reading novels","classical music","photography"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":77,"level":"high"},"conscientiousness":{"score":76,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":94,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":12,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":99,"level":"high"}}}

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "When it comes to fake content on social media, I believe the most effective way is to stay curious but not too trusting. As a man who's been around for quite some time, I've learned that you can't just take everything at face value because people nowadays are quick to spread misinformation online. It started with my granddaughter showing me funny videos from TikTok when she visits us in Bari, and then it turned into seeing ads that promised easy money if I click on them—nonsense! But being an electrician, even part-time these days, taught me the importance of checking things carefully before relying on them. My father only went to primary school, so he always told me not to jump into conclusions too fast; he’d say you need time to think it over properly.",
  "reasoning_summary": "First, I considered how social media can be misleading for someone like myself who isn’t as tech-savvy as younger generations. Then, drawing from my electrician experience, which requires precision and double-checking connections, I realized that dealing with online content should also involve verifying facts step-by-step. Since my neuroticism score is high, I tend to worry about things—like when I see fake news or scams—and so caution becomes part of my natural approach. Being curious helps me want to dig deeper even if it means asking others for help. My hobbies such as reading novels and classical music also make me more aware because literature often deals with truthfulness in stories, while classical music requires attention to detail.",
  "tone": "Pragmatic yet skeptical",
  "citations": [],
  "safety_notes": "None provided by user, but I always remind people that social media can be dangerous if not handled carefully—especially for someone my age who might get tricked easily.",
  "used_persona_fields":["age", "gender", "occupation", "education_level", "psychological_feature", "time_onsocialmedia", "hobbies", "ocean"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: When it comes to fake content on social media, I believe the most effective way is to stay curious but not too trusting. As a man who's been around for quite some time, I've learned that you can't just take everything at face value because people nowadays are quick to spread misinformation online. It started with my granddaughter showing me funny videos from TikTok when she visits us in Bari, and then it turned into seeing ads that promised easy money if I click on them—nonsense! But being an electrician, even part-time these days, taught me the importance of checking things carefully before relying on them. My father only went to primary school, so he always told me not to jump into conclusions too fast; he’d say you need time to think it over properly.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: First, I considered how social media can be misleading for someone like myself who isn’t as tech-savvy as younger generations. Then, drawing from my electrician experience, which requires precision and double-checking connections, I realized that dealing with online content should also involve verifying facts step-by-step. Since my neuroticism score is high, I tend to worry about things—like when I see fake news or scams—and so caution becomes part of my natural approach. Being curious helps me want to dig deeper even if it means asking others for help. My hobbies such as reading novels and classical music also make me more aware because literature often deals with truthfulness in stories, while classical music requires attention to detail.
response_parsed.safety_notes: None provided by user, but I always remind people that social media can be dangerous if not handled carefully—especially for someone my age who might get tricked easily.
